---
title: "Rating Curve Lifecycle"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Rating Curve Lifecycle}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

A rating curve translates a measured **stage** (water level, *h*) into **flow**
(*Q*) using the power-law equation applied per limb:

$$Q = C(h - a)^b$$

Ratings move through three governance tiers — **Bronze → Silver → Gold** —
before being applied to produce operational flow records.

---

## Tiers

| Tier | Purpose | Mutability |
|---|---|---|
| Bronze | As-received from source (WISKI, consultant, model output) | Immutable once saved |
| Silver | QC-reviewed: continuity checked, limb boundaries verified | Reviewable |
| Gold | Formally approved for production use | Authoritative |

Each tier maintains its own independent version sequence. A site can have
Bronze v1, Silver v1, and Gold v1 simultaneously — these are distinct entries
in the register.

---

## Lifecycle Steps

### 1. Construction

A `RatingCurve` is assembled from a table of limbs. Each limb covers a stage
range and carries its own `C`, `a`, `b` coefficients and a `doubtful` flag
(used to suppress plotting of extrapolated out-of-bank limbs).

```
Limb 1:  lower = 0.0 m,  upper = 0.5 m
Limb 2:  lower = 0.5 m,  upper = 1.2 m   <- boundaries must be contiguous
Limb 3:  lower = 1.2 m,  upper = Inf      <- doubtful = TRUE (out of bank)
```

Multiple `RatingCurve` objects with non-overlapping validity windows are
grouped into a `RatingSet` to represent a site's rating history.

---

### 2. Continuity Check

At each limb junction, both limbs must produce the same Q.
`check_limb_continuity()` reports any gaps:

```
  junction h_junction Q_lower Q_upper gap_m3s gap_pct connected
1      1|2       0.50   12.30   11.87    0.43    3.5%     FALSE
2      2|3       1.20   48.10   48.10    0.00    0.0%      TRUE
```

A gap greater than 1% is considered a discontinuity. If one exists the rating
is not fit for production use.

---

### 3. Continuity Correction

`fix_limb_continuity()` resolves discontinuities by adjusting the `C`
coefficient of each limb above the anchor (limb 1), while preserving `a` and
`b`. Changes are propagated upward so all junctions connect cleanly.

---

### 4. Bronze — Save As-Received

The uncorrected, original rating is saved to Bronze unchanged. This preserves
the raw source for audit purposes.

- Assigned `rating_id`: e.g. `510310_RATING_bronze_v1`
- `status`: `Active`
- `method_of_receipt`: e.g. `"WISKI export"`, `"consultant report"`
- `derivation_method`: e.g. `"spot gaugings"`, `"hydraulic model"`, `"mixed"`
- `model_or_project`: e.g. `"TUFLOW_Phase2_2023"` (if applicable)

---

### 5. Silver — QC Review

After continuity correction and any limb boundary adjustments, the reviewed
curve is promoted to Silver. Bronze is left untouched.

- New version number scoped to the Silver tier
- Previous Silver `Active` version auto-superseded
- `status` of the prior version updated to `Superseded` in the register

---

### 6. Gold — Approval

Once the Silver curve has been signed off, it is saved to Gold. This is the
version used by `apply_rating()` to produce operational flow data.

- Only one `Active` version per site per tier at any time
- Supersession is automatic on save

---

### 7. Application to Level Data

`apply_rating()` accepts a `Level_Daily` or `Level_15min` object and a
`RatingCurve` or `RatingSet`. Each observation is matched to the correct
curve by date, rated, and returned as `Flow_Daily` or `Flow_15min`. A
`doubtful` column propagates the extrapolation flag.

---

### 8. Provenance Register

Every save writes a row to `register/rating_register.csv`:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `rating_id` | Unique ID: `{site_id}_RATING_{tier}_v{version}` |
| `site_id` | Station identifier |
| `tier` | `bronze` / `silver` / `gold` |
| `status` | `Active` or `Superseded` |
| `version` | Integer, scoped per site + tier |
| `valid_from` / `valid_to` | Rating validity window |
| `n_limbs` | Number of limbs |
| `source` | Free-text origin label from the curve |
| `method_of_receipt` | How the file or data was received |
| `derivation_method` | How the rating was developed (see below) |
| `model_or_project` | Model name, project, or team attribution |
| `saved_by` | Username |
| `saved_at` | Timestamp |
| `notes` | Free-text annotation |

#### Derivation methods

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `"spot gaugings"` | Fitted from field velocity-area measurements |
| `"hydraulic model"` | Derived from HEC-RAS, ISIS, TUFLOW, etc. |
| `"statistical model"` | Regression or statistical extrapolation |
| `"mixed"` | Gaugings used to calibrate or constrain a model |

---

### 9. Physical Storage

```
<root>/
  bronze/hydrometric/ratings/ratings.parquet   <- all sites, all bronze versions
  silver/hydrometric/ratings/ratings.parquet   <- all sites, all silver versions
  gold/hydrometric/ratings/ratings.parquet     <- all sites, all gold versions
  register/rating_register.csv                 <- provenance register (all tiers)
```

One Parquet file per tier holds all sites and versions stacked by row (one row
per limb). `site_id` + `version` is the composite key within each file.

---

## Summary

```
Field survey / model output
        |
        v
   [RatingCurve]
        |
        +-- check_limb_continuity()
        |         |
        |         v  (if gaps found)
        |   fix_limb_continuity()
        |
        +-- save_rating(tier = "bronze")   ->  bronze/hydrometric/ratings/
        |
        +-- save_rating(tier = "silver")   ->  silver/hydrometric/ratings/
        |         (after QC review)
        |
        +-- save_rating(tier = "gold")     ->  gold/hydrometric/ratings/
                  (after sign-off)
                       |
                       v
              apply_rating(level_obj, rs)
                       |
                       v
                 Flow_Daily / Flow_15min
```

See `vignette("rating-curve-lifecycle")` for worked R code covering each step.
